multiplechoiceresponse.label: You connect to a robot and the signal meter shows 1 bar. What should you do?

choicegroup.choice: Start the session immediately — 1 bar is enough
Reduce your movement speed and proceed
Wait for signal to improve before starting — poor signal means laggy controls and bad data
Restart the robot to improve signal

solution.p: Poor signal causes control lag, which creates inconsistent data. Always wait for at least 3 bars before starting a session.

multiplechoiceresponse.label: What does the red STOP button do?

choicegroup.choice: Ends the recording session
Disconnects from the robot
Sends an immediate stop command — the robot stops all movement
Resets the robot to home position

solution.p: The STOP button sends an emergency stop command. The robot stops moving immediately. Use it whenever you're unsure — it's always safe to stop.

multiplechoiceresponse.label: Why does smooth movement matter for a TeleOp Controller?

choicegroup.choice: It makes the robot look better on camera
Smooth movements create clean training data — the AI learns smooth, consistent behavior from your demonstrations
Smooth movement is faster than jerky movement
It reduces battery consumption

solution.p: Your movements are the training data. Jerky, inconsistent movements teach the AI jerky, inconsistent behavior. Smooth, deliberate movements teach the AI smooth, reliable behavior.

multiplechoiceresponse.label: You're mid-session and the battery drops to 12%. What should you do?

choicegroup.choice: Continue — 12% is enough to finish
End the session now — stop before the battery hits 10% to avoid an incomplete episode
Speed up to finish the task faster
Ignore it — the platform will stop automatically

solution.p: Always end sessions before 10% battery. A robot that dies mid-task creates an incomplete episode — wasted data that can't be used for training.

multiplechoiceresponse.label: Which session name follows good naming conventions?

choicegroup.choice: session1
test april 17
arm-pick-red-cube-table-001
my session

solution.p: Good session names describe the robot type, task, environment, and sequence number. This makes sessions findable and usable by trainers reviewing the data.

multiplechoiceresponse.label: What is the purpose of speaking task descriptions during a session?

choicegroup.choice: It helps you remember what you're doing
It's required by the platform
Voice annotations create language-paired data that enables training language-conditioned AI policies
It improves the video quality

solution.p: VLA models need (image, language, action) triples. Speaking task descriptions while operating creates the language component of this training data.

multiplechoiceresponse.label: You review a session and notice the robot stood still for 3 seconds in the middle. What should you do?

choicegroup.choice: Keep it — 3 seconds is not a big deal
Mark it for discard — idle time teaches the AI to do nothing
Edit out the idle section
Submit it anyway and let the trainer decide

solution.p: Idle trajectories are one of the most common data quality problems. The AI learns from your movements — if you stood still, it learns to stand still. Discard episodes with significant idle time.
